Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The green object moved up again. Let me check its current position and see if it's close to any of the target boxes. Let me also check if SPACE now works when near a box.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Current green(N) and white(W):")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'W'):
        print(f"  {n['color']}: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nTarget boxes (c with b children):")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'c':
        print(f"  c: boundary={n['boundary']}, children={n['children']}")

# Check if green is near any box
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        green_r = n['boundary'][0][0]
        green_c = n['boundary'][0][1]
        print(f"\nGreen top-left: ({green_r}, {green_c})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Hmm, let me check the pattern again. After the first UP, it was at rows 45-47 (3 rows tall). After the second UP, it's at rows 41-43 (3 rows tall). So it moved up 4 rows but kept the same shape.
Wait, but the first move (RIGHT) changed shape from 3x4 to 4x3, and the second move (UP) changed it from 4x3 to 3x4. But the third move (UP again) kept it at 3x4.
So maybe the rotation only happens when changing direction? Or maybe the shape alternates with each move?
